---
title: "AI Careers"
crossref:
  lst-title: "🎯 Checkpoint 6."
  lst-labels: alpha a
format: live-html
sidebar: lesson-6
---

```{=html}
<style>
/* Career section headers */
h2 {
  padding-left: 14px;
  margin-top: 2rem;
}

/* Hook sentence immediately after each h2 */
h2 + p {
  font-style: italic;
  color: #444;
  margin-left: 4px;
  margin-bottom: 0.6rem;
}

/* h3 headings inside callouts */
.callout-body h3 {
  color: #00746f;
  font-size: 1rem;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #d0ece9;
  padding-bottom: 3px;
  margin-top: 1.3rem;
}

/* Pill-style jump links */
.ltorange-container {
  width: 100%;
  margin-left: 0;
  margin-right: 0;
}

.ltorange-container h3 {
  margin-top: 0;
}

/* Pill-style jump links */
.ltorange-container a {
  display: inline-block;
  background: #e8f5f4;
  border: 1px solid #00746f;
  border-radius: 20px;
  padding: 3px 11px;
  margin: 3px 4px;
  text-decoration: none;
  font-size: 0.9em;
  color: #00746f !important;
  line-height: 1.8;
}
.ltorange-container a:hover {
  background: #00746f;
  color: white !important;
}
</style>
```

## How can you use AI in your future career?

::: {.ltorange-container}
### **Jump to a Career**
[🐄 Animal Agriculture](#animal-agriculture)
[⚡ Electrician](#electrician)
[🏥 Healthcare & Nursing](#healthcare-nursing)
[❄️🔥 HVAC](#hvac)
[🌱 Plant Agriculture](#plant-agriculture)
[🚰 Plumbing](#plumbing)
[🐾 Veterinarian](#veterinarian)
[💧 Water Quality Scientist](#water-quality-scientist)
:::

## 🐄 Animal Agriculture {#animal-agriculture}

Sensors attached to livestock — video cameras, microphones, ear tags — can catch early signs of illness before a farmer would notice anything is wrong.

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="AI in animal agriculture"}

### How could AI help you?
The AI watches breathing patterns, movement, and feeding behavior around the clock. When something looks off, it flags that animal for a human to check — catching problems days earlier than visual inspection alone.

### How is AI Machine Learning used here?
**Classification** — the AI predicts a label (example: "healthy" vs "needs attention").

**Regression** — the AI predicts a number (example: body temperature estimate or weight gain trend).

:::{.ltorange-container}
**See how AI is reshaping work in animal agriculture.**

["AI isn't likely to wipe out all farming jobs—but it is changing who bears the risks"](https://theconversation.com/ai-isnt-likely-to-wipe-out-all-farming-jobs-but-it-is-changing-who-bears-the-risks-275227)  
*We suggest watching the video.*

["'It's already here:' How AI is transforming livestock farmer operations"](https://www.cargill.com/story/artificial-intelligence-in-animal-farming)

["After growing up on a dairy farm, this Peter Thiel-backed founder is using AI to save cattle ranching."](https://fortune.com/2026/04/13/halter-ceo-craig-piggott-peter-thiel-ai-cowgorithm/)
:::

### How could you use your expertise to train AI?
AI trained in a lab doesn't know what a heat-stressed cow looks like in August in East Tennessee. That's where you come in:

- Label real examples of healthy vs sick animals and describe what *early* warning actually looks like.
- Add context — feed changes, seasonal stress, herd history — so alerts match real conditions, not just textbook cases.

### How could you define rules for AI?
- Lower alert thresholds during heat waves so the system catches problems sooner.
- No treatment decisions without a human check, especially for urgent alerts.

### How could you work toward this career?
Build your foundation with Biology, Health Science, Algebra, and Statistics. Hands-on experience matters — look for farm internships, 4‑H programs, or volunteer opportunities with large-animal vets.

### Where can you pursue this career in East Tennessee?
**UT Extension — Agriculture**
<https://utextension.tennessee.edu/ut-extension-master-programs/>

**Walters State Community College**
<https://ws.edu/academics/programs/ttp/as/agriculture-animal-science.aspx>
:::

---

## ⚡ Electrician {#electrician}

A smart sensor on an electrical panel can detect overheating or abnormal current patterns — before a fire starts.

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="AI in electrical work"}

### How could AI help you?
AI watches voltage, current, and temperature data continuously and flags conditions that a technician should inspect. Faults that might go unnoticed for weeks become visible in the data within hours.

### How is AI Machine Learning used here?
**Classification** — the AI predicts a label (example: "safe" vs "unsafe," "normal" vs "fault").

**Regression** — the AI predicts a number (example: temperature estimate, load estimate, or failure risk score).

:::{.ltorange-container}
**See how AI is reshaping work in the electrical industry.**

["Artificial Intelligence in the Electrical Industry"](https://www.rogerselectric.com/resources/artifical-intelligence-electrical-industry/)  

🎥 ["How the AI Boom is Creating Electrician Jobs for Young Workers"](https://youtu.be/LAC7Mi9epvw?si=KZcUS_Fitl7f_AT_)
:::

### How could you use your expertise to train AI?
You know what a safe installation looks like and what "urgent" means on a real jobsite — that's knowledge an AI can't get from data alone. Labeling real examples of safe/unsafe setups, and defining the line between "keep watching" and "shut it down now," is exactly what makes an AI system trustworthy.

### How could you define rules for AI?
- Alert a human immediately when danger is detected.
- AI is a diagnostic tool, not a clearance. Standard safety procedures stay in place regardless of what the model says.

### How could you work toward this career?
CTE and shop courses build the hands-on foundation. After graduation, registered apprenticeship programs let you earn while you learn the full trade.

### Where can you pursue this career in East Tennessee?
**Walters State Community College**
<https://careertraining.ws.edu/training-programs/residential-electrician/>

**TCAT Morristown - Industrial Electricity**
<https://tcatmorristown.edu/programs/industrial-electricity>

**TVA — Electrician Apprentice Program**
<https://www.tva.com/Careers/Electrician-Apprentice-Program>

**ApprenticeshipTN**
<https://www.tn.gov/apprenticeshiptn/find-a-program.html>
:::

---

## 🏥 Healthcare & Nursing {#healthcare-nursing}

AI-assisted imaging can scan an X‑ray for potential fractures in seconds — giving the care team a head start before anyone's even opened the file.

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="AI in healthcare & nursing"}

### How could AI help you?
In clinical settings, AI acts as a second set of eyes. It reviews images, flags patterns, and surfaces cases that need closer attention — so nurses and physicians can focus their time where it matters most.

### How is AI Machine Learning used here?

| ML type | What it predicts | Example |
|---|---|---|
| **Classification** | a label | "fracture" vs "no fracture" |
| **Regression** | a number | estimated healing time, complication risk score |

:::{.ltorange-container}
**See how AI is reshaping work in healthcare.**

🎥 ["How AI is transforming healthcare across the country"](https://www.nbcnews.com/video/how-ai-is-transforming-healthcare-across-the-country-251919941722)  

["Could AI nursing robots help health care staffing shortages?"](https://www.cnn.com/2025/09/12/tech/taiwan-nursing-robots-nurabot-foxconn-nvidia-hnk-spc)  

:::

### How could you use your expertise to train AI?
Clinical AI is only as good as its training data — and that data has to be labeled by people who know medicine.

- Provide correctly labeled examples (X‑rays, records, lab values) including the *hard* cases: unusual presentations, patients outside the typical range.
- Add the clinical context the model can't see, like symptom history and mechanism of injury.

### How could you define rules for AI?
**AI doesn't make the final call — a person does.** No matter how confident the model looks, a healthcare professional reviews before acting. Extra scrutiny applies for high-risk populations and any case where symptoms and the AI's output don't line up.

### How could you work toward this career?
Take Biology, Health Science, Algebra, and Statistics. Volunteer at clinics, hospital programs, or community health events whenever you get the chance — hands-on exposure matters for both applications and learning.

### Where can you pursue this career in East Tennessee?
**University of Tennessee, Knoxville — BSN**
<https://nursing.utk.edu/undergraduate-programs/bachelor-of-science-in-nursing-bsn/>

**East Tennessee State University — BSN**
<https://www.etsu.edu/bachelor/nursing.php>

**Tusculum University — Pre-Medical Concentration**
<https://catalog.tusculum.edu/preview_program.php?catoid=8&poid=1737&returnto=1133>

**TCAT Morristown - Practical Nursing**
<https://tcatmorristown.edu/programs/practical-nursing>

**Walters State Community College**
<https://ws.edu/academics/programs/aas/nursing.aspx>
:::

---

## ❄️🔥 HVAC (Heating & Cooling) {#hvac}

Most HVAC failures leave warning signs in the data days before the system actually breaks down.

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="AI in HVAC"}

### How could AI help you?
By watching temperature readings, airflow rates, and energy consumption over time, AI can recognize when a system is trending toward failure — a clogged filter, a failing motor, a refrigerant issue. A technician gets dispatched before the customer even notices a problem.

### How is AI Machine Learning used here?
**Classification** — the AI predicts a label (example: "normal airflow" vs "blocked airflow").

**Regression** — the AI predicts a number (example: expected temperature drop, energy use, or time-to-failure estimate).

:::{.ltorange-container}
**See how AI is reshaping work in the HVAC Industry.**

🎥 ["Meet the Next-Gen Nest Learning Thermostat"](https://youtu.be/iJ7ZZ1Xy5HU?si=x90mxPteENUIZbl6)  

["ChatGPT-Style AI Tool for HVAC Takes Aim at Labor Shortage"](https://www.achrnews.com/articles/print/153649-chatgpt-style-ai-tool-for-hvac-takes-aim-at-labor-shortage)  

:::

### How could you use your expertise to train AI?
- Show the AI what normal looks like across different makes, models, seasons, and building types — because "normal" varies a lot.
- Provide before/after examples from real repairs so it learns to connect the warning pattern to the actual problem.

### How could you define rules for AI?
- Alert for **extreme temperature risks** and prioritize rapid response.
- Flag safety issues immediately and require a human technician to verify before any action is taken.

### How could you work toward this career?
HVAC is one of the best trades for apprenticeship — you can enter a certificate program right out of high school and be working in the field quickly.

### Where can you pursue this career in East Tennessee?
**Walters State Community College**
<https://careertraining.ws.edu/training-programs/hvacr-technician/>

**TCAT Morristown - Heating, Ventilation, Air Conditioning, Refrigeration**
<https://tcatmorristown.edu/programs/heating-ventilation-air-conditioningrefrigeration>

**ApprenticeshipTN**
<https://www.tn.gov/apprenticeshiptn/find-a-program.html>
:::

---

## 🌱 Plant Agriculture {#plant-agriculture}

A photo taken on a phone can give an AI-powered app enough information to identify early blight or nutrient deficiency — before the damage is visible to the naked eye.

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="AI in plant agriculture"}

### How could AI help you?
AI systems trained on images of healthy and diseased crops can scan photos faster than any scouting team. Some systems go further, estimating yield from aerial imagery or predicting harvest timing based on weather and growth data.

### How is AI Machine Learning used here?
**Classification** — the AI predicts a label (example: "healthy" vs "diseased," or the specific disease type).

**Regression** — the AI predicts a number (example: disease severity score, expected yield, or days until harvest).

:::{.ltorange-container}
**See how AI is reshaping work in plant agriculture.**

["How AI Can Impact Agriculture"](https://www.ffa.org/technology/how-ai-can-impact-agriculture/)  

🎥  ["A new generation of smart robots could take over farm tasks once handled by humans"](https://youtu.be/vy7VxJCnBCw?si=4kj2rzAe-KyGwft6)  

:::

### How could you use your expertise to train AI?
- Collect and label images across seasons, lighting conditions, and growth stages — a disease looks different on a seedling than on a mature plant.
- Connect disease IDs to research-based treatment options. A recommendation is only useful if it matches how farmers actually work.

### How could you define rules for AI?
- Any **pesticide recommendation** requires human review and must follow research-based guidance.
- Use **early alerts** to prevent crop loss, but track false alarms so you don't end up over-treating healthy fields.

### How could you work toward this career?
Work in gardens and on farms whenever possible. Join **4‑H**. Take biology and data courses. Practical experience with crops is something no classroom can fully replace.

### Where can you pursue this career in East Tennessee?
**East Tennessee State University - Plant Science**
<https://www.etsu.edu/cas/biology/academics/plantscience.php>

**UT Extension — Agriculture**
<https://utextension.tennessee.edu/ut-extension-master-programs/>

**Walters State Community College**
<https://ws.edu/academics/programs/ttp/as/agriculture-plant-soil-science.aspx>
:::

---

## 🚰 Plumbing {#plumbing}

Water is expensive to lose and expensive to repair — AI helps catch leaks before a slow drip becomes a flooded basement.

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="AI in plumbing"}

### How could AI help you?
Sensors measuring flow rate, pressure, and acoustic signatures can detect the early signs of a leak or blockage. AI learns what "normal" sounds and feels like in a system, so it can flag the moment something changes.

### How is AI Machine Learning used here?
**Classification** — the AI predicts a label (example: "leak" vs "no leak," "normal" vs "abnormal flow").

**Regression** — the AI predicts a number (example: estimated leak rate, pressure drop amount, or probability of failure over the next 30–90 days).

:::{.ltorange-container}
**See how AI is reshaping work in plumbing.**

["Your plumber has a new favorite tool: ChatGPT"](https://www.cnn.com/2025/10/10/tech/ai-chatgpt-blue-collar-jobs)  

🎥  ["SewerAI AutoCode Demo"](https://youtu.be/InVX7jDerzM?si=aj6mPHWxmAbG2Zpm)  

:::

### How could you use your expertise to train AI?
Real training data comes from real systems. Labeled examples of normal vs abnormal readings — captured at the time of a confirmed leak or clog — are what make a model accurate in the field rather than just in testing.

### How could you define rules for AI?
- Shut off water (or trigger urgent alerts) in true emergencies.
- Alert a human quickly enough to prevent flooding and property damage.

### How could you work toward this career?
Apprenticeship programs are the primary path — you earn while you learn. Look for registered programs through ApprenticeshipTN or start with a foundations certificate.

### Where can you pursue this career in East Tennessee?

**Walters State Community College**
<https://careertraining.ws.edu/training-programs/plumbing-foundations/>

**TCAT Morristown - Pipe Fitting and Plumbing Technology**
<https://tcatmorristown.edu/programs/pipefitting-and-plumbing-technology>

**ApprenticeshipTN**
<https://www.tn.gov/apprenticeshiptn/find-a-program.html>
:::

---

## 🐾 Veterinarian {#veterinarian}

From flagging abnormal lab values to sorting urgent from routine cases, AI can help a busy clinic stay on top of every patient.

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="AI in veterinary medicine"}

### How could AI help you?
Veterinary clinics generate a lot of data — records, images, lab panels, weight histories. AI can surface patterns a busy staff might miss: a value trending in the wrong direction, an imaging result that warrants a closer look, a patient overdue for follow-up.

### How is AI Machine Learning used here?
**Classification** — the AI predicts a label (example: "possible infection" vs "no infection," or "urgent" vs "routine").

**Regression** — the AI predicts a number (example: recovery time estimate, weight-change trend, or risk score).

:::{.ltorange-container}
**See how AI is reshaping work in veterinary medicine.**

🎥 ["AI helps vets pinpoint X-ray abnormalities"](https://youtu.be/KkjgmZP8DFs?si=3jusBBpkyv3WwK2_)  

["Artificial intelligence poised to transform veterinary care"](https://www.avma.org/news/artificial-intelligence-poised-transform-veterinary-care)  

:::

### How could you use your expertise to train AI?
The challenge in veterinary medicine is variation — dogs, cats, cattle, and horses don't look the same on an X-ray, and a healthy value for one breed isn't healthy for another. Getting AI right requires labeled examples across species, breeds, ages, and body conditions.

### How could you define rules for AI?
Any medication or dosing guidance from an AI system gets verified by a licensed veterinarian. Emergency cases always get human review, regardless of what the model says.

### How could you work toward this career?
Volunteer with animals early — shelters, farms, and vet clinics all need help, and that experience is invaluable. Build a strong foundation in Biology and Chemistry.

### Where can you pursue this career in East Tennessee?
**University of Tennessee, Knoxville — Veterinary Medicine**
<https://vetmed.tennessee.edu/>

**Tusculum University — Veterinary Concentration**
<https://catalog.tusculum.edu/preview_program.php?catoid=8&poid=1737&returnto=1133>

**Walters State Community College — Veterinary Concentration**
<https://ws.edu/academics/programs/as/animal-science-pre-veterinary-medicine.aspx>
:::

---

## 💧 Water Quality Scientist {#water-quality-scientist}

You've been using sensors to track nitrate levels this week — water quality scientists are the people building the AI that makes sense of that data at scale, across hundreds of sites at once.

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="AI in water quality science"}

### How could AI help you?
Sensors in rivers, lakes, and distribution systems collect continuous readings of pH, turbidity, nitrates, dissolved oxygen, and more. AI watches for patterns that fall outside what's normal for that location and season — and alerts scientists before a problem reaches the public.

### How is AI Machine Learning used here?
**Classification** — the AI predicts a label (example: "normal" vs "possible contamination event").

**Regression** — the AI predicts a number (example: predicted pH, turbidity, nitrate level, or contamination risk score).

:::{.ltorange-container}
**See how AI is reshaping work in hydrology.**

["Applying Machine Learning to Harmful Algal Blooms"](https://www.earthdata.nasa.gov/news/blog/applying-machine-learning-harmful-algal-blooms)  

["First-of-its-Kind Monitoring Tool Uses AI to Forecast Water Contamination"](https://www.usu.edu/today/story/first-of-its-kind-monitoring-tool-uses-ai-to-forecast-water-contamination)  

:::

### How could you use your expertise to train AI?
The buoy you've been studying is a good example of this: sensors collect the raw signal, but a scientist's knowledge shapes what gets labeled as "safe" and what gets flagged. Paired datasets — sensor readings plus lab-confirmed results — are what give a model something trustworthy to learn from.

### How could you define rules for AI?
AI flags the anomaly; it doesn't issue the public health advisory. Confirmatory lab testing and clear escalation procedures are required before any action that affects public safety.

### How could you work toward this career?
Chemistry, Biology, and Environmental Science are your core classes. Water-testing projects through school clubs or local stream monitoring programs give you real field experience before college.

### Where can you pursue this career in East Tennessee?
**UTK — Environmental Engineering (Tickle College of Engineering)**
<https://tickle.utk.edu/academics-and-programs/undergraduate-programs/environmental-engineering-bs/>

**Tusculum University — Environmental Science**
<https://site.tusculum.edu/environmental-science/>

**East Tennessee State University - Biology with a Concentration in Natural Resource Ecology**
<https://catalog.etsu.edu/preview_program.php?catoid=65&poid=18984>

**Walters State Community College**
<https://ws.edu/academics/programs/as/pre-environmental-health.aspx>
:::
